query: PostgreSQL TimescaleDB Citus real-time analytics 50000 events per second sub 100ms latency
answer: Citus and TimescaleDB can handle 50,000 events per second with sub-100ms latency for real-time analytics. They use parallel processing and advanced data aggregation techniques. Both platforms scale PostgreSQL for high-performance analytics.

results.title: Real-Time Analytics & Massively Parallel Postgres - Citus Data
results.snippet: Customer-facing analytics dashboards often require sub-second response times. Because Citus parallelizes SQL over multiple nodes, Citus can provide real-time responsiveness, even with terabytes of data. Moreover, Citus can pre-aggregate the data in parallel across all servers using standard SQL commands to enable millisecond query times even with billions of events per day. Because of the intelligent way Citus distributes SQL over multiple nodes, you get massive parallelism AND concurrency, with Citus coordinating hundreds or even thousands of cores for high performance data ingest and fast analytical queries. #### Get Insanely Fast Analytics Without Re-Architecting Your App. One of our customers describes Citus as insanely fast for real-time analytics (yay.) There are other benefits as well. ## Why Citus for Real-Time Analytics Dashboards? 2. Need to enable customers to analyze new events & new data as it happens, in real-time. ## Learn More About Citus & Real-Time Analytics Apps. Real-time event aggregation at scale using Postgres w/Citus.

results.title: How to Implement Real-Time Analytics with TimescaleDB - OneUptime
results.snippet: Learn how to implement real-time analytics with TimescaleDB, covering hypertable setup, continuous aggregates, real-time data ingestion.

results.title: Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres
results.snippet: # Timescale 2025: Scaling Real-Time Analytics in Postgres. Timescale unveils innovations for scaling Postgres for real-time analytics. ## Scaling Postgres for Real-Time Analytics on Time-Series Data: The Challenge. Postgres is one of the most powerful and flexible databases available, but as real-time analytics workloads grow, traditional approaches start to fail. To date, we’ve made Postgres the best database for real-time analytics—eliminating the need for specialized infrastructure. Now, in 2025, we’re pushing even further—removing bottlenecks, reducing query latency, and proving that Postgres can scale to petabytes of data and trillions of metrics—all in a single instance. This week, we’re unveiling the next wave of innovations that make Postgres even more powerful for real-time analytics. This was one of the most requested features for TimescaleDB—and on Thursday, we’ll show you how it can supercharge your real-time analytics workflows. Just Postgres for real-time analytics at scale. By the end of the week, you’ll have a complete picture of how Timescale eliminates bottlenecks, scales to petabytes, and brings the speed of transactional databases to analytics workloads.
